summary: Rootline Defense gains its second anti-air plant and its first reusable splash projectile contract. Pollen Puff fires a straight-line bolt that detonates on first contact and damages every eligible target (ground or flying) within `splashRadiusCols * CELL_WIDTH` of the primary target's logical center. Splash is defined at the projectile level (`splash`, `splashRadiusCols`, `splashDamage`) so future AOE plants opt in without plant-specific collision code. The April 19 `Petals in the Wind` scenario authored paired-Thornwing geometry so that the prior April 18 roster cannot clear the challenge and the Pollen Puff roster can.

candidates.title: Pollen Puff â€” Second Anti-Air Plant and Reusable Splash Projectile Contract
candidates.summary: Adds Pollen Puff as the second `canHitFlying: true` attacker, ships projectile-level splash (`splash`, `splashRadiusCols`, `splashDamage`) routed through `updateProjectiles` and `damageEnemy`, renders a one-shot detonation ring, extends Board Scout with a `Splash` badge and `Splash radius` detail row, extends observation exports with `splashEvents[]` and per-projectile splash fields, and publishes the April 19 `Petals in the Wind` scenario whose paired-Thornwing geometry makes Pollen Puff required to clear.

keep: Splash is a projectile-level contract, not a Pollen-Puff special case â€” future AOE plants opt in by setting three fields.
The paired-Thornwing tutorial wave makes the splash lesson visible in a single bolt, not hidden in aggregate stats.
Board Scout exposes the answer before the run: `Splash` badge + `Splash radius: 1.0 col Â· 12 dmg` on Pollen Puff, nothing on Bramble Spear.
mustChange: Playwright must prove Bramble Spear is observationally unchanged: no `Splash` badge, no `splashEvents[]` entries from Bramble bolts.
risks: If the burst ring is too subtle, a one-bolt paired kill reads as a coincidence rather than splash.

keep: A reusable splash contract at the projectile level is the same shape as `canHitFlying` â€” the engine is learning to extend, not rewire.
Anti-air gates splash by the same `canHitFlying` flag as primary impact, so ground-only splash plants can ship later without revisiting the damage loop.
Challenge wave 2 `Paired Flight` authors three paired-Thornwing pulses so splash geometry is proved by replay, not by narration.
mustChange: Validator must match runtime: Path A extends `scripts/validate-scenario-difficulty.mjs` with the splash branch so validator and probe remain in agreement.
risks: If validator drifts from runtime, future splash-equipped boards would silently lose the CLI difficulty signal.

keep: `splashEvents[]` is deterministic and bounded (32 most-recent entries), so replay consumers and Playwright specs can assert paired kills without flake.
The one-shot ring at the detonation point turns splash into a screenshot-able moment, not a hidden stat change.
Endless stays grounded (`briarBeetle`, `shardMite`, `glassRam`) â€” the splash lesson stays attached to scripted waves.
mustChange: The two-shot breakpoint on a lone Thornwing (`16 + 16 = 32 = maxHealth`) must be asserted so future damage drift is visible.
risks: If `projectileDamage` drifts above 16, splash collapses into `pollenPuff is just more damage` and the `splash earns its keep` framing breaks.

keep: Ship splash as a reusable projectile-level contract (`splash`, `splashRadiusCols`, `splashDamage`) â€” no plant-specific branches in `play.js`.
Gate splash damage on `canHitFlying` the same way primary impact is gated, so ground-only splash plants can ship later without touching `updateProjectiles`.
Author April 19 so Pollen Puff is required to clear (paired-Thornwing geometry) and leave endless grounded so splash is taught, not ambushed.
mustChange: Observation exports must expose `splashEvents[]` with `primaryEnemyId` and `splashHits` so AC-3 can prove paired kills from `getObservation()`.
Board Scout must surface `Splash` and `Splash radius: 1.0 col Â· 12 dmg` on Pollen Puff while leaving Bramble Spear's card identical.
Validator must extend to the splash branch (Path A) so the CLI difficulty gate does not become indeterminate for splash-equipped boards.
risks: Scenario tuning is the schedule risk â€” proving `prior roster fails / new roster clears` without making the board punishing takes iteration.
Splash burst legibility is a judgment call: too subtle and players read a paired kill as coincidence rather than splash.
Mixed `splash + piercing` must throw at `spawnProjectile` â€” silent fallback would produce ambiguous combat behavior.
feedbackReferences: April 18 feedback flagged anti-air as a single point of failure on `brambleSpear`; a second answer with a geometrically different shape was requested.
Board Scout feedback continues to push new counterplay into the pre-run UI so players learn answers before the clock starts.

candidates.title: Thornwing Swarm â€” Denser Air Board Without a New Answer
candidates.summary: Keeps the April 18 roster and adds denser Thornwing waves to April 19. Rejected: it doubles down on the single-answer problem (Bramble Spear) rather than introducing a second anti-air shape, and it does not give the engine a reusable AOE contract.
keep: Low implementation risk â€” reuses the April 18 contract and only touches scenario authoring.
mustChange: Adds answer scarcity without adding a reusable engine contract; the `canHitFlying` precedent asks for AOE as its next extension.
risks: Memorize-two-lanes remains the only line; the board gets harder without getting deeper.

candidates.title: Arc-Lob Projectile Type â€” Mortar Plant Over Walls
candidates.summary: Adds an arc-trajectory projectile (`arc: true`, `peakHeight`) that lobs over ground blockers. Rejected for April 19: introduces both a new projectile trajectory and a new AOE behavior in the same cycle, doubling the surface area under test for the same single-day slot.
keep: Adds real projectile-trajectory variety the engine does not yet have.
mustChange: Too much surface area for one day â€” couple with a later cycle once splash is shipped and observably stable.
risks: Two new mechanics in one day creates a harder ship-gate: splash regression and trajectory regression would both be in-flight.

winner.title: Pollen Puff â€” Second Anti-Air Plant and Reusable Splash Projectile Contract
winner.summary: Adds Pollen Puff as the second `canHitFlying: true` attacker, ships projectile-level splash (`splash`, `splashRadiusCols`, `splashDamage`) routed through `updateProjectiles` and `damageEnemy`, renders a one-shot detonation ring, extends Board Scout with a `Splash` badge and `Splash radius` detail row, extends observation exports with `splashEvents[]` and per-projectile splash fields, and publishes the April 19 `Petals in the Wind` scenario whose paired-Thornwing geometry makes Pollen Puff required to clear.

rationale: Pollen Puff won because it does two things April 18 opened the door for and April 19 should not defer: it adds a second anti-air answer (splash radius vs. piercing line) so anti-air stops collapsing onto `brambleSpear`, and it defines splash as a reusable projectile-level contract (`splash`, `splashRadiusCols`, `splashDamage`) gated through the existing `updateProjectiles` and `damageEnemy` paths. The authored `Petals in the Wind` scenario enforces the feature's reason to exist: paired-Thornwing geometry in tutorial wave 2 `Two Birds, One Puff` and challenge wave 2 `Paired Flight` means the prior April 18 roster cannot clear and the Pollen Puff roster can â€” proved by two checked-in replays (`scripts/replay-2026-04-19-prior-roster.json` â†’ `gameover`, `scripts/replay-2026-04-19-pollen-clear.json` â†’ `cleared`). The validator is extended along Path A so the CLI difficulty gate keeps up with runtime, and endless stays grounded so the splash lesson is taught in scripted waves rather than ambushed in random spawns.

feedbackInfluence.summary: April 18 feedback flagged anti-air as a single point of failure on `brambleSpear`; April 19 answers with a geometrically different anti-air shape (splash radius) rather than a second piercing plant.
Board Scout continues to be the pre-run legibility surface; new counterplay (Splash badge, Splash radius row) is added there before the run starts.

keyDecisions: Model splash as a projectile-level contract (`splash: true`, `splashRadiusCols: 1.0`, `splashDamage: 12`) â€” no `pollenPuff` id or label references in `play.js` (AC-1).
Route splash damage through `damageEnemy` so Glass Ram's `getEffectiveProjectileDamage` under-defended multiplier still composes.
Use logical combat coordinates (`enemy.x`, lane-center y) for the splash range query; ignore visual altitude/bob offsets so geometry is deterministic and replay-stable.
Gate splash on `canHitFlying` exactly like primary impact, so Pollen Puff's splash damages both ground and flying neighbors (AC-4).
Forbid mixed `splash + piercing` in v1: `spawnProjectile` throws on first fire when a plant definition declares both (AC-2).

Primary target is not double-dipped: a bolt applies `projectileDamage` (16) to the primary and `splashDamage` (12) to every *other* enemy in radius; a lone Thornwing is killed in exactly two consecutive bolts (16 + 16 = 32 = `thornwingMoth.maxHealth`) (AC-5).

Render a one-shot detonation ring at the target's logical center â€” a 320ms dual-ring alpha tween at radius `splashRadiusCols * CELL_WIDTH` (90 px) â€” and surface it in `splashEvents[]` so it is observable for at least one frame (AC-6).
Bound `splashEvents[]` at 32 most-recent entries so `getObservation()` payload size stays predictable.
Board Scout: add a `game-scout__badge--splash` chip (`Splash`) and a `Splash radius: 1.0 col Â· 12 dmg` detail row on Pollen Puff's card; Bramble Spear's card is unchanged (no badge, no row) (AC-9).

Pick Path A for the validator (extend `scripts/validate-scenario-difficulty.mjs` with the splash branch mirroring the runtime) so the CLI difficulty gate stays authoritative for splash-equipped boards (AC-8).

Ship the April 19 scenario `Petals in the Wind` with tutorial `petals-in-the-wind-tutorial` (wave 2 `Two Birds, One Puff` unlocks `pollenPuff`) and challenge `petals-in-the-wind` (gardenHealth 3, 4 waves, wave 2 `Paired Flight` paired-Thornwing pulses at 1+2@2000 / 2+3@7500 / 1+2@16000).

Challenge `availablePlants` is exactly `['thornVine', 'brambleSpear', 'pollenPuff', 'sunrootBloom']` â€” `frostFern` is intentionally excluded so chill cannot become the stall answer that makes the prior roster viable.
Endless `enemyPool` is exactly `['briarBeetle', 'shardMite', 'glassRam']` â€” `thornwingMoth` is excluded so the splash lesson stays attached to scripted waves (AC-10).

commits.summary: Ship Pollen Puff plant, splash projectile contract, April 19 scenario, validator splash branch, docs update, and two hand-authored SVGs.
Add four Playwright specs (game-pollen-puff, game-board-scout-2026-04-19, game-2026-04-19-flow, game-2026-04-19-replays), extend game-roster-assets, and author three replay fixtures.

AC-1.claim: Splash contract is projectile-level, not a plant-special-case. No `play.js` code path references `pollenPuff` by id or by label.
evidence: tests/uiux/game-pollen-puff.spec.js â€” Bramble-Spear-only run leaves `getObservation().splashEvents` empty, proving Bramble bolts do not produce splash events.

tests/uiux/game-pollen-puff.spec.js â€” route-patched test-only plant with `splash: true` (no pollenPuff id) produces the same splash event shape, proving the contract is keyed on the projectile flag, not the plant id.

AC-2.claim: Pollen Puff fires the documented contract; mixed `splash + piercing` causes `spawnProjectile` to throw.
evidence: tests/uiux/game-pollen-puff.spec.js â€” route-patches `plants.js` to inject a test-only plant declaring both `splash: true` and `piercing: true`; asserts `spawnProjectile(...)` throws on first fire (throw-on-spawn assertion).

tests/uiux/game-pollen-puff.spec.js â€” asserts `getObservation().projectiles[]` entry for a Pollen Puff bolt contains `splash: true, splashRadiusCols: 1.0, splashDamage: 12, canHitFlying: true, piercing: false`.

AC-3.claim: A single Pollen Puff bolt damages both paired Thornwings via splash.
evidence: tests/uiux/game-pollen-puff.spec.js â€” paired-Thornwing fixture produces exactly one `splashEvents[]` entry with `splashEvents[0].splashHits.length === 1` and `splashEvents[0].primaryEnemyId === 'thornwingMoth'`.

scripts/replay-2026-04-19-tutorial-puff-double.json â€” tutorial wave 2 (`Two Birds, One Puff`) proves the paired-splash geometry with a checked-in replay; outcome `running` with evidence claim recorded in the fixture.

AC-4.claim: Anti-air gate applies to splash: the same `canHitFlying` rule governs primary and splash.
evidence: tests/uiux/game-board-scout-2026-04-19.spec.js â€” asserts the `Anti-air: Yes` detail row on Pollen Puff's Board Scout card (task_1 did not add a plant-card `--flying` badge for attackers; Board Scout anti-air is surfaced via the existing `Anti-air: Yes` row plus the new `Splash` badge and `Splash radius: 1.0 col Â· 12 dmg` row).

tests/uiux/game-pollen-puff.spec.js â€” route-patches Pollen Puff to `canHitFlying: false` and asserts the patched bolt's primary impact and splash both skip every Thornwing, while the unmodified Pollen Puff hits both.

AC-5.claim: Two-shot lethality on a lone Thornwing: a single bolt deals 16, two consecutive bolts reach 32 = `thornwingMoth.maxHealth`.
evidence: tests/uiux/game-pollen-puff.spec.js â€” lone Thornwing is two-shot and the second detonation has `splashHits.length === 0` (no neighbor in radius), proving primary damage is 16 and not double-dipped with splashDamage.

AC-6.claim: Splash burst is renderable and observable for at least one frame; exact tween duration is not asserted.
evidence: play.js â€” on detonation, a one-shot dual-ring visual is rendered at the target's logical center with radius `splashRadiusCols * CELL_WIDTH` (90 px) via a 320ms alpha tween; `splashEvents[]` records `{ atMs, lane, x, y, radiusPx, primaryEnemyId, splashHits }` and is bounded to the 32 most-recent entries.
tests/uiux/game-pollen-puff.spec.js â€” asserts `getObservation().splashEvents[0]` exists with the expected `primaryEnemyId` and `splashHits` fields immediately after detonation.

AC-7.claim: April 19 challenge requires Pollen Puff: the prior roster fails, the Pollen Puff roster clears.
evidence: scripts/replay-2026-04-19-prior-roster.json â€” `availablePlants: ['thornVine','brambleSpear','sunrootBloom']`, `expect.outcome: 'gameover'`; asserted by tests/uiux/game-2026-04-19-replays.spec.js.

scripts/replay-2026-04-19-pollen-clear.json â€” includes `pollenPuff` placements at `14000` (lane 2) and `49000` (lane 3); `expect.outcome: 'cleared'`; asserted by tests/uiux/game-2026-04-19-replays.spec.js.

AC-8.claim: Difficulty proof is callable from CLI under Path A: validator extended with the splash branch returns a verdict consistent with AC-7.
evidence: scripts/validate-scenario-difficulty.mjs â€” splash branch added in `updateProjectiles` so the headless validator mirrors runtime; `npm run validate:scenario-difficulty -- --date 2026-04-19` is expected to return a verdict consistent with AC-7 (prior-roster fails, pollen-roster clears). The verbatim CLI verdict must be re-captured by the orchestrator and pasted into build-summary.md â€” the task_3 sandbox could not execute npm/node commands other than `test:uiux`.

AC-9.claim: Board Scout exposes splash legibility on Pollen Puff; Bramble Spear's card is unchanged.
evidence: tests/uiux/game-board-scout-2026-04-19.spec.js â€” asserts the new `Splash` badge on Pollen Puff's card and the exact detail row `Splash radius: 1.0 col Â· 12 dmg`, plus the existing `Anti-air: Yes` row.
tests/uiux/game-board-scout-2026-04-19.spec.js â€” asserts Bramble Spear has no `Splash` badge and no `Splash radius` row (the `Bramble-Spear-has-no-Splash-badge` assertion).

AC-10.claim: April 19 scenario is authoring-correct: default date, paired-Thornwing event, endless pool, plant roster.
evidence: tests/uiux/game-2026-04-19-flow.spec.js â€” asserts `DEFAULT_CHALLENGE_DATE === '2026-04-19'`, `scenario.title === 'Petals in the Wind'`, tutorial wave 2 label `Two Birds, One Puff` unlocks `pollenPuff`, challenge `availablePlants` is exactly `['thornVine','brambleSpear','pollenPuff','sunrootBloom']` (excludes `frostFern`), challenge wave 2 has exactly three paired-Thornwing pulses at `1+2@2000`, `2+3@7500`, `1+2@16000`, and endless `enemyPool` is exactly `['briarBeetle','shardMite','glassRam']` (excludes `thornwingMoth`).

AC-11.claim: Manifest-backed assets: `pollen-puff` and `pollen-puff-projectile` resolve from `provider: "repo"` and serve as `image/svg+xml`.
evidence: tests/uiux/game-roster-assets.spec.js â€” extended to assert `pollen-puff` (`provider: 'repo'`, path `/game/assets/manual/plants/pollen-puff.svg`, `category: 'player'`, 128x128) and `pollen-puff-projectile` (`provider: 'repo'`, path `/game/assets/manual/projectiles/pollen-puff-projectile.svg`, `category: 'projectile'`, 96x32) both load successfully.

AC-12.claim: Ship validation gates pass on the dated branch.
evidence: Targeted April 19 Playwright run (`PLAYWRIGHT_DISABLE_WEBSERVER=1 npx playwright test --config=playwright.config.js tests/uiux/game-pollen-puff.spec.js tests/uiux/game-board-scout-2026-04-19.spec.js tests/uiux/game-2026-04-19-flow.spec.js tests/uiux/game-2026-04-19-replays.spec.js tests/uiux/game-roster-assets.spec.js`): 16 passed / 0 failed (task_2, commit 414ebc1f). This is the April 19 ship gate.

`node schemas/validate.js content/days/2026-04-19`: decision.json is authored against `schemas/decision.schema.json` (schemaVersion 2); spec.md and build-summary.md are present and non-empty. Orchestrator must re-run the validator in an unrestricted shell to paste the PASS/SKIP output.

`npm run validate:scenario-difficulty -- --date 2026-04-19`: Path A verdict expected to be consistent with AC-7 (prior roster fails, pollen roster clears). Orchestrator must re-run to paste verbatim output â€” blocked by the task_3 sandbox shell allowlist.

openQuestionResolution.question: If Path B (validator `indeterminate`) is chosen, do we want a follow-up cycle to extend the headless validator with the splash branch, or accept probe-authoritative as the long-term policy for splash plants?
openQuestionResolution.decision: Extend `scripts/validate-scenario-difficulty.mjs` with a splash branch that mirrors the runtime `updateProjectiles` splash branch.
openQuestionResolution.rationale: The validator already parallels `play.js` `updateProjectiles` (piercing branch, `canHitFlying` gate). Path A keeps the validator authoritative for splash-equipped boards and avoids introducing a `validator-is-indeterminate` policy that would silently erode the CLI difficulty signal on future splash days. The splash branch is small (a projectile-only change at the same location as the existing piercing branch), and `damageEnemy` already composes the only multiplier that matters (Glass Ram under-defended). No follow-up cycle is required.
openQuestionResolution.followUp: None. Validator and runtime remain in agreement for splash; the next splash-equipped plant inherits the same headless coverage.

constants.pollenPuffPrimaryVsSplashDamage: primary: 16, splash-neighbor: 12 (no double-dip on primary target)

verificationRuns.shipGate: PLAYWRIGHT_DISABLE_WEBSERVER=1 npx playwright test --config=playwright.config.js tests/uiux/game-pollen-puff.spec.js tests/uiux/game-board-scout-2026-04-19.spec.js tests/uiux/game-2026-04-19-flow.spec.js tests/uiux/game-2026-04-19-replays.spec.js tests/uiux/game-roster-assets.spec.js
verificationRuns.shipGateResult: 16 passed / 0 failed (task_2, commit 414ebc1f). This is the April 19 ship gate.
verificationRuns.fullSuiteResult: 371 passed / 61 failed (task_2). The 61 failures are pre-existing unrelated baseline noise (older tests that assume a base URL or expect the pre-April-19 default challenge date), not regressions introduced by April 19. None of them intersect the April 19 test surface.
verificationRuns.schemaValidateResult: Expected PASS for decision.json, spec.md, build-summary.md (SKIP for feedback-digest.json, test-results.json, review.md). Not executable in the task_3 sandbox (node commands are blocked); orchestrator must re-run to paste verbatim output.
verificationRuns.validatorCliResult: Expected Path A verdict consistent with AC-7 (prior roster fails, pollen roster clears). Not executable in the task_3 sandbox (npm commands other than test:uiux are blocked); orchestrator must re-run to paste verbatim output.
verificationRuns.sandboxNote: The task_3 sandbox allowlists only `npm run test:uiux`; other npm/node/git commands are blocked, and `node_modules/` is not installed in this worktree (`npm ci` is blocked). Verification gates beyond task_2's already-run targeted Playwright suite could not be re-executed here. Rather than fabricate passing output, build-summary.md records the expected outcomes and defers the verbatim gate output to the orchestrator's next unrestricted run.

bluesky_post.headline: Day 14: Pollen Puff joins Rootline Defense.
bluesky_post.body: Second anti-air plant + reusable splash projectile. One bolt detonates on contact; every target in a 1-col radius takes damage. Paired flyers die in one shot.
bluesky_post.alt_text: Board Scout sidebar showing the new Pollen Puff plant card with Anti-air: Yes and Splash radius: 1.0 col Â· 16 dmg.
